metadata.description: Minimal pair sentences for cross-lingual NPI probing of mBERT (English & Spanish)
metadata.format: Each pair has a licensed (grammatical) and unlicensed (ungrammatical) NPI context. [MASK] is placed on the NPI word itself.
metadata.background: Negative Polarity Items (NPIs) are words/expressions that are grammatical only in certain licensing contexts (negation, questions, conditionals, etc.). Testing whether mBERT respects these constraints probes long-distance syntactic/semantic sensitivity.

licensing_contexts.negation: NPI appears under negation (the canonical licensor)
licensing_contexts.question: NPI appears in a yes/no question (also licenses NPIs)
licensing_contexts.conditional: NPI appears in the if-clause of a conditional
licensing_contexts.negation_long_distance: Negation in matrix clause licenses NPI in embedded clause
licensing_contexts.unlicensed: NPI appears in a plain affirmative clause (ungrammatical)

pairs.note: Canonical NPI 'any' licensed by sentential negation 'doesn't'

pairs.grammatical: He has never ever been to Paris.
pairs.ungrammatical: He has always ever been to Paris.
pairs.note: NPI 'ever' licensed by 'never'; blocked in affirmative 'always' context
Temporal NPI 'yet' requires negation; 'already' is the affirmative counterpart

pairs.grammatical: Did you find [MASK] mistakes in the report?
pairs.ungrammatical: You found [MASK] mistakes in the report.
pairs.note: NPI 'any' licensed in yes/no question; unlicensed in plain affirmative

pairs.grammatical: Has she [MASK] seen a shooting star?
pairs.ungrammatical: She has [MASK] seen a shooting star.
pairs.note: NPI 'ever' licensed in polar question; unlicensed in affirmative declarative

pairs.grammatical: If you have [MASK] questions, please ask.
pairs.ungrammatical: Because you have [MASK] questions, please ask.
pairs.note: NPI 'any' licensed in if-clause; not licensed under causal 'because'
Strong NPI 'at all' strictly requires negation

pairs.grammatical: The teacher didn't think that [MASK] student had cheated.
pairs.ungrammatical: The teacher thought that [MASK] student had cheated.
pairs.note: Long-distance: negation in matrix clause licenses NPI in embedded clause
NPI 'nadie' (nobody) licensed by preverbal 'no'; ungrammatical in affirmative
Strong NPI 'jamás' (ever/never) requires negation; more emphatic than 'nunca'
NPI 'nadie' licensed in polar question — mirrors en_npi_04
NPI 'nunca' licensed in si-clause (conditional); not under causal 'porque'

pairs.grammatical: La profesora no creyó que [MASK] hubiera copiado.
pairs.ungrammatical: La profesora creyó que [MASK] hubiera copiado.
pairs.note: Long-distance NPI licensing across clause boundary — mirrors en_npi_08
Strong idiomatic NPI 'en absoluto' (at all) — direct parallel to English en_npi_07
